documentation.readme: Converts PE so that it can be then injected just like a normal shellcode. (At the same time, the output file remains to be a valid PE). Supports both 32 and 64 bit PEs
usage_sections: TLS callbacks (called once, before the Entry Point is executed)
exceptions (if the executable you converted will be run as a shellcode, and throw the exception, the appropriate exception handler will not be found, and the application will crash)
Delay Load Imports (only the basic Import Table support is implemented)
MUI files (if the executable you converted expects some elements of the GUI have to be loaded from a MUI file, it won't work)

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --scope
arguments.description: Scope filter (URL pattern, IP range, or domain).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the c2 category. Fits when the engagement needs: Converts PE so that it can be then injected just like a normal shellcode. (At the same time, the output file remains to be a valid PE). Supports both 32 and 64 bit PEs.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: Converts PE so that it can be then injected just like a normal shellcode. (At the same time, the output file remains to be a valid PE). Supports both 32 and 64 bit PEs.
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: git clone --recursive https://github.com/hasherezade/pe_to_shellcode.git. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `pe2shc.exe <path to your PE> [output path*]`. Further invocations from upstream: `runshc.exe <converted file>`. Converts PE so that it can be then injected just like a normal shellcode. (At the same time, the output file remains to be a valid PE). Supports both 32 and 64 bit PEs.

name: pe_to_shellcode
summary: Converts PE so that it can be then injected just like a normal shellcode. (At the same time, the output file remains to be a valid PE). Supports both 32 and 64 bit PEs

trust.warning: Attribution / index entry only. The operator must audit the code, the README, the license, and the releases before invoking. Phase 5 — entry is auto-generated from the operator's curated fetch list; no automated enrichment yet.

use_cases: When you need c2, use pe_to_shellcode because Converts PE so that it can be then injected just like a normal shellcode. (At the same time, the output file remains to be a valid PE). Supports both 32 and 64 .
When you need a concrete invocation, run `pe2shc.exe <path to your PE> [output path*]` as documented upstream for pe_to_shellcode.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `runshc.exe <converted file>` as documented upstream for pe_to_shellcode.
When chaining c2 tools, pair pe_to_shellcode with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `pe_to_shellcode --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
